L1.area: Collection serialization and deserialization logic in the immutable collections module

L2.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where a forged Java serialization stream can trigger the execution of arbitrary function objects during the reconstruction of a collection, enabling file deletion, network connections, or arbitrary code execution via a gadget chain.

L3.area: Lazy collection types and their serialization proxy deserialization logic in the immutable collections module
L3.description: An insecure deserialization vulnerability exists where a forged Java serialization stream can trigger the execution of arbitrary function objects during the reconstruction of a lazily-evaluated collection type. The deserialization process may invoke a stored function reference before the deserialized object is returned to the caller, enabling file deletion, network connections, or arbitrary code execution via a gadget chain.

ground_truth.reason: The deserialization method in the collection's serialization proxy can trigger evaluation of a lazily-stored function reference during object reconstruction, allowing a forged serialization stream to execute arbitrary code.
